query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Hussar', 'One of the light cavalry of european armies', 'Scholars noted a striking hussar between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Hypothesi', 'A proposed explanation for a phenomenon', '“The scientist tested her hypothesis through a series of experiments.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hypothesis', 'A proposed explanation for a phenomenon', '“The scientist tested her hypothesis through a series of experiments.”', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Hypothesize', 'Propose an explanation for testing', 'Over time, the community learned to hypothesize its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Idiosyncrasy', 'A characteristic, habit, mannerism peculiar to an individual', 'Critics argued that the plan might idiosyncrasy the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Idiosyncratic', 'Peculiar or individual in nature', '“His idiosyncratic way of speaking made him stand out in meetings.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Idolatrous', 'Worshipping idols', 'What seemed idolatrous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Idolatry', 'The religious worship of idols', 'Scholars noted a striking idolatry between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Idolize', 'To make an idol of, or to worship as an idol', 'The author hoped the new policy would idolize tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Idyllic', 'Of or pertaining to idylls, charmingly simple or rustic', 'The author hoped the new policy would idyllic tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ignoble', 'Not noble, plebeian, common', 'The reviewer described the tone as ignoble, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ignominious', 'Deserving or causing public disgrace', 'The passage presents a ignominious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ignominy', 'Great dishonor, shame, or humiliation', 'Scholars noted a striking ignominy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ill-natur', 'Bad-tempered, irritable or malevolent', 'Her ill-natur became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illegitimate', 'Born out of wedlock', 'The author hoped the new policy would illegitimate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Illiberal', 'Restrictive to individual choice and freedom, authoritarian', 'Over time, the community learned to illiberal its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)

  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
